---
title: "CN334 Data Analytics Lab Assignment"
author: "Aakriti Sharma"
date: "2026-03-19"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Exercise 1
#Plot

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
plot(1:10)
plot(rnorm(10), 1:10)
```
# commands
# What is my current directory
```{r}
getwd()
```
#To see folders or files in the working directory
```{r}
dir()
dir(pattern = ".txt")
```
# Managing and accessing folders in R
```{r}
# check if the directory or file exisits
file.exists("colonJan13")
# Create a directoryfolder
dir.create("colonJan13")
setwd("colonJan13")
```

# For cross-platform compatibility, its best to use file.path() to create paths. for example
```{r}
wkdir <- getwd()
newdir <- "MyNewDir"
subdir <- "Project1"
newdirPath <- file.path(wkdir, newdir, subdir)
if (file.exists(wkdir)) dir.create(newdirPath)
dir(pattern = "My")
```

# Path expand
```{r}
path.expand("~")
myhome <- path.expand("~")
newdir <- file.path(path.expand("~"), "Rwork", " colonJan13")
```

# view OR change of home directory
```{r}
Sys.getenv("HOME")
Sys.setenv(HOME = "path") 
```

# demo examples
```{r}
demo(graphics)
demo(nlm)
```
# Getting help with functions and features
```{r}
## help or ? will open a help page
help(lm)
# ?matrix
`?`(matrix)
## The functions find() or apropos() will search for a command
apropos("mean")
## The function example will run example of a command
example(rep)
```

# keyword search (If you don’t know the command)
```{r}
help.search("combination")
help.start()
```

# R as a big calculator
```{r}
2 + 2
2 * 2
2 * 100/4
2 * 100/4+ 2
2 * 100/(4 + 2)
2^10
log(2)
```

# store the value of (tmpVal=log(2))
```{r}
tmpVal <-log(2)
print(tmpVal)
tmpVal
exp(tmpVal)
```

# Elementary commands
```{r}
2 * 5^2
x <-2 * 5^2
print(x)
```
# Assignment operators are:<-,=,->
```{r}
2 * 5^2
y <-2 * 5^2
z <-2 * 5^2
z <-2 * 5^2
print(y)
x== y
y== z
```

# generating random numbers 
```{r}
rnorm(5)
```

# Basic Operators
# Comparison operators
```{r}
x<-5
y<-9
# equal 
x == y 
# not equal
x != y
# greater than
x > y
# less than 
x < y
#greater than or equal
x >= 5
#less than or equal
y <= 10
```

# Logical operators
#AND
```{r}
x <- 1:10
y <- 10:1
x > y & x > 5
```

# OR
```{r}
x == y | x != y
```

# NOT
```{r}
!x > y
```

# Data set in R
```{r}
data(women)
ls()
ls(pattern = "w")
```

# Rsessions (workspace) and saving session history
```{r}
save(women, file = "women.RData")
save.image(file = "entireL2session.RData")
rm(women)
ls(pattern = "women")
load("women.RData")
ls(pattern = "women")
```

#To view most recent R commands in a session
```{r}
history()
help(history)
history(100)
history(pattern = "save")
savehistory(file = "L2.Rhistory")
```
